🧠 Neural Network Comparison

MNIST test accuracy — all 4 models

🛠 Tools & Technologies
Python 3.11scikit-learnpandas numpymatplotlibseaborn TensorFlow / KerasPyTorchstatsmodels NLTKBeautifulSouprequests Kaggle Notebooks
Standardised Feature Distributions

All 7 features after StandardScaler (μ=0, σ=1). Price shows right-skew (1.23); discrete features (bathrooms, rooms) show bimodal patterns.

KDE Density Plots with Skewness

KDE curves with annotated skewness values. Features with skew > 0.5 benefit from log-transformation before regression.

Box Plots — Post-Preprocessing

Price and adress features contain notable outliers beyond the whiskers, consistent with the right-skewed distributions.

Pearson Correlation Matrix
Feature PairrInterpretation
image_links ↔ details0.78Agencies with more photos write richer descriptions
bathrooms ↔ rooms0.71Larger properties have more of both
area ↔ rooms0.52More rooms → more area (expected)
price ↔ bathrooms0.53Key price driver
price ↔ rooms0.41Moderate price influence

Dataset
Iris
150 samples, 3 classes
Setosa AUC
1.00
Perfectly separable
Model Comparison
ModelAccuracyPrecisionRecallF1-Score
Logistic Regression0.9670.9670.9670.967
Random Forest0.9670.9670.9670.967
SVM (RBF)1.0001.0001.0001.000
Key Insights
  • All models perform near-perfectly — Iris is clean, balanced, and small
  • The only classification challenge is Versicolor vs Virginica (petal features resolve it)
  • Setosa is perfectly linearly separable from all other species
  • StandardScaler was fitted on train data only — no data leakage
  • Stratified split ensures equal class distribution in both splits
Optimal k Selection — Elbow Method & Silhouette Score

Elbow visible at k=3–4. Silhouette peaks at k=2 but k=3 gives the best business interpretability. Final: k=3.

Customer Segments — PCA 2D Projection

3 clusters in PCA space. Overlap reflects genuine complexity — true separation lives in higher dimensions.

Cluster Feature Profiles & Churn Rate
ClusterProfileChurn RateAction
0Low usage, few service calls~9%Upsell campaigns
1Moderate usage, intl plan~12%Monitor closely
2High day minutes, high churn~21%Retention priority
Hourly Post Volume — Overview
Time Series Decomposition (Trend / Seasonality / Residual)
Moving Average Comparison (SMA, WMA)
Exponential Smoothing Models

Holt-Winters Additive achieves best RMSE (0.19). Multiplicative fails (RMSE=2236) due to near-zero values.

ACF & PACF — ARIMA Order Selection

Rapid ACF decay → low autocorrelation. PACF cut-off at lag 1 → p=1 sufficient.

SARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,0)[24] — 7-Day Test Forecast

RMSE = 0.23 — best of all models. Captures 24-hour daily cycle.

🔄 Model Selector

Note: The prediction runs a fully client-side neural network simulation trained on MNIST statistics. The probability distribution is computed from the digit geometry using handcrafted features that closely approximate the model outputs.
